name: Desertview
description_short: Desertview Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative serving Emery County residents with savings accounts, loans, and online banking.
description_long:
Desertview Federal Credit Union traces its roots to Castle Valley Federal Credit Union, established in 1960 as a community-chartered institution. In 1981, it merged with American Coal Credit Union and adopted its current name, beginning with just over $1 million in assets. The credit union operates as a direct descendant of the first credit unions established in rural Germany over 150 years ago, maintaining the cooperative philosophy of pooling member resources for mutual financial benefit. Today, it serves as a leading financial cooperative throughout Emery County.

Desertview offers a range of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, share certificates, new vehicle loans, online banking with bill payment capabilities, direct deposit setup, debit/ATM cards, loan applications, and membership enrollment. Members can access online banking services, view current rates, and apply for loans through their website. The credit union provides both digital and traditional banking channels to serve its membership.

As a federally-insured credit union, Desertview distinguishes itself through NCUA insurance protecting all savings to at least $250,000 per member account. The institution emphasizes member ownership and democratic participation—members vote on Board of Directors elections, attend annual meetings, and maintain lifelong membership status once joined. The credit union explicitly positions itself as offering higher returns on savings and lower loan rates compared to traditional banks, rooted in its non-profit cooperative structure.

Desertview's primary limitation is geographic eligibility; membership requires living or working in Emery County, making it inaccessible to most consumers outside this rural Utah region. The website provides minimal specific rate information (new vehicle loans show "Please call" rather than posted APRs), and service details are relatively sparse compared to larger financial institutions. For qualified members in Emery County, however, the institution offers stable, member-owned financial services with strong regulatory backing.
pros: NCUA insurance protecting savings to at least $250,000 per member account
Member-owned cooperative structure with voting rights on Board of Directors elections
Lifelong membership status once joined—members retain membership permanently
Emphasis on lower loan rates and higher savings returns compared to traditional banks
Multiple access channels including online banking with bill payment, debit/ATM cards, and direct deposit
Community-focused institution with 60+ year history serving Emery County
Invites members to annual meetings for direct input on credit union decisions
cons: Geographic eligibility restricted to Emery County residents or employees only
Limited rate transparency on website—new vehicle loans require phone call for APR quotes
Minimal product and service descriptions available online compared to larger credit unions
No mention of overdraft protection, credit cards, or extended loan products beyond vehicle loans
Small institution with potentially limited branch locations and service hours
best_for: Emery County residents seeking member-owned financial services with democratic participation
Local employees looking for credit union membership with community focus and strong safety backing
Members prioritizing cooperative principles and wanting voting rights in financial institution governance
Savers seeking NCUA-insured accounts with emphasis on member returns over corporate profits
diagnosis: Desertview Federal Credit Union is best for Emery County residents and employees seeking member-owned financial services with democratic participation, NCUA insurance protection, and competitive rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility—you must live or work in Emery County to qualify for membership, making this institution inaccessible to most consumers outside this rural Utah region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
